findings.title: Unconditional closesocket call on invalid socket handle
findings.rationale: The diff removed the check for `socket_ != INVALID_SOCKET` before calling `closesocket`. Windows API requires a valid socket handle; passing `INVALID_SOCKET` (typically -1) causes `closesocket` to return `SOCKET_ERROR` (WSAENOTSOCK) without crashing, but violates the API contract. The original check prevented this invalid call.
findings.suggestion: Reintroduce the check to avoid invalid API calls

evidence.ref: Windows API documentation: closesocket requires valid socket handle
spdlog coding guidelines: avoid invalid API calls
